text:
## Headline

Nimbus management used its earnings call to reiterate fiscal 2026 revenue guidance and raise its full-year non-GAAP operating margin outlook while detailing the pending Cirrus Analytics acquisition.

## Q&A signals

- Asked about Cirrus integration timing, CEO Daniel Cho said "native Cirrus query capabilities to be generally available inside the Stratus console by the end of fiscal Q4 2026" [Q1].
- On the margin guide raise, CFO Helena Vargas attributed "about 60 basis points to the hiring slowdown" and "about 40 basis points to underlying gross-margin improvement in Stratus" [Q2].
- On hyperscaler competition, Cho noted "win rate against the hyperscaler-native offerings remained above 75 percent in Q2 in competitive deals" [Q7].

## Commitments

- Management expects to close the Cirrus Analytics acquisition by the end of fiscal Q3 2026, which will "materially expand our footprint in real-time log analytics" [K1].
- Full-year fiscal 2026 revenue guidance was reiterated, with the company "reiterating our previously communicated full-year fiscal 2026 revenue guidance of 2.55 to 2.60 billion dollars" [K2].
- Non-GAAP operating margin guidance was raised, with Nimbus now expecting it "to land at approximately 19 percent, up from our prior guidance of 18 percent" [K3].
